mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon regardless of underlying economic growth.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative during identified tightening phases, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances following a >5% monthly drop in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand, which expands corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which lowers input costs and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, accelerating real GDP growth drives earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, but this effect reverses during overheating phases.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth accelerates beyond 3.5% annualized, or Rank IC drops below zero during identified recovery windows.
